feed.title: Google Cloud: Deep Learning VM Images
feed.subtitle: Changelog for Google Cloud product: Deep Learning VM Images

entry.content: Images with Nvidia 570 drivers have been deprecated as Nvidia officially stopped supporting these versions. You can use images with Nvidia 580 drivers instead.
entry.summary: Nvidia has deprecated images with 570 drivers due to the end of official support. Users are advised to switch to images with Nvidia 580 drivers for continued compatibility and performance.
entry.title: Breaking: Nvidia driver update announced

entry.content:
M132 release

  • Ubuntu 22.04 and Ubuntu 24.04 images with Nvidia 580 drivers and CUDA 12.9 stack are now available for Common and PyTorch 2.9 images.
entry.summary: Ubuntu images now include updated Nvidia 580 drivers and the CUDA 12.9 stack. These are available for both Common and PyTorch 2.9 images on Ubuntu 22.04 and 24.04.
entry.title: Feature: New Nvidia Drivers and CUDA Stack Available

entry.content:
The following framework versions have reached their end of patch and support dates:

  • Base versions with CUDA 12.4 and earlier
  • Tensorflow versions 2.17 and earlier
  • PyTorch versions 2.3 and earlier

To view the end of patch and support dates, see Supported framework versions.

For more information on what the end of patch and support date means, see Support policy schedule.

If you must use an image after deprecation against Google security recommendations and at your own risk, see After deprecation.
entry.summary: Frameworks including CUDA 12.4, Tensorflow 2.17, and PyTorch 2.3 have reached their end of support. For more details on dates and implications, refer to the supported frameworks and support policy schedule documentation.
entry.title: Breaking: End of Support for Framework Versions
